channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Putin announces an Easter ceasefire as Russia and Ukraine swap hundreds of POWs
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin announced a temporary Easter ceasefire in Ukraine starting Saturday, citing humanitarian reasons, as Russia and Ukraine swapped hundreds of captured soldiers in the largest exchange since Moscow's full-scale invasion started over three years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former RNC chief questions Trump, Vance on 'unearned smugness' around Abrego Garcia case
item.description: Former Republican National Committee (RNC) chair Michael Steele raised an eyebrow Friday about the Trump administration's dedication to preventing Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a mistakenly deported man held in El Salvador, from returning to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: American pastor kidnapped in South Africa thanks God after being rescued
item.description: American pastor Josh Sullivan says his "personal relationship with Jesus" helped him survive terrifying kidnapping ordeal that he calls "the worst experience.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin calls for 'Easter truce' with Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has called for an “Eastern truce” with Ukraine, directing the Kremlin’s military to temporarily pause fighting until Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Tunisian opposition figures sentenced to up to 66 years in prison
item.description: Tunisian media say a court has sentenced leading opposition figures to between 13 and 66 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Romania's presidential election redo is a high-stakes test of its democracy following annulled vote
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romania's presidential election redo next month will be a high-stakes test for the European Union member’s democracy after last year’s annulled vote triggered the country’s deepest political crisis since 1989. Romania’s Constitutional Court annulled the election on Dec.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and NICOLAE DUMITRACHE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the tensions between Iran and the US before their second round of talks
item.description: Iran and the United States will hold talks Saturday in Rome, their second round of negotiations over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Four centuries later, Easter's date remains divisive. Some church leaders want that to change
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — For more than 400 years, Catholic and Orthodox churches have used different ways to determine the date of Easter. But this Sunday will mark a special moment for Christians, as the churches celebrate of Jesus’ resurrection on the same day.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, COLLEEN BARRY and LEFTERIS PITARAKIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 25 in Gaza and Huckabee makes first appearance as US ambassador
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes across Gaza killed at least 25 people on Friday including children, hospital workers said, as the new U.S. ambassador to Israel made his first public appearance in Jerusalem.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran and the US move to expert-level talks over Tehran's nuclear program after negotiations in Rome
item.description: Iran and the United States will begin having experts meet to discuss details of a possible deal over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Crews restore power to nearly all customers after island-wide blackout hit Puerto Rico
item.description: Following an island-wide blackout that hit Puerto Rico earlier in the week after a transmission line failed, power was restored on Friday to more than 98% of customers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says US will 'take a pass' on Ukraine peace efforts if Russia refuses to play ball
item.description: The Trump administration made clear it will abandon peace attempts for Ukraine if Russia fails to adhere to any terms, but the president did not say whether there would be consequences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-ICE director says Abrego Garcia will be 'deported again' if returned to US
item.description: A retired acting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) director said Kilmar Abrego Garcia, the Maryland man who was deported to the terrorist confinement center (CECOT) in El Salvador, will be removed again if he is approved to return to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Worshippers return to restored Notre Dame for Easter rituals with emotion and awe
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Four months after its long-awaited reopening, Notre Dame Cathedral welcomed worshippers and tourists alike on Friday for an emotional Easter ceremony centered on the Crown of Thorns, a circular band of branches encased in a golden tube and one of Christianity’s most revered relics.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Three tourists among 4 killed after Italian cable car crashes to the ground near Naples
item.description: Three tourist are among the four dead after a cable car crash in Italy. Local prosecutors have opened an investigation into possible manslaughter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US man hijacks small plane in Belize, stabs 3 people before he's shot dead
item.description: Authorities at the Philip S.W. Goldson International Airport reported a hijacking of a small plan by a U.S. man who stabbed two passengers and a pilot before being fatally shot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Protest letters from former Israeli soldiers lay bare profound rifts over the ongoing war
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — When nearly 1,000 Israeli Air Force veterans signed an open letter last week calling for an end to the war in Gaza, the military responded immediately, saying it would dismiss any active reservist who signed the document.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands of Gaza children are malnourished under Israel's food blockade, aid groups say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Aid groups are raising new alarm over Israel’s blockade of the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, where it has barred entry of all food and other goods for more than six weeks.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, FATMA KHALED and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US to withdraw 600 troops from Syria, leaving fewer than 1,000 to help counter IS militants
item.description: The U.S. troops have been critical not only in the operations against the Islamic State but as a buffer for the Kurdish forces against Turkey.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Transgender women in Britain fear ruling could place toilets, sports and hospitals off limits
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Transgender women will be excluded from women’s toilets, hospital wards and sports teams after a U.K.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump takes center stage in Canada’s prime minister election debate
item.description: Canada's electoral debate for the next prime minister focused on who is best equipped to counter President Trump amid his tariff war and his 51st state comments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICC opens inquiry into Hungary for failing to arrest Netanyahu
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — Judges at the International Criminal Court want Hungary to explain why it failed to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu when he visited Budapest earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building in China, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli troops will remain in Gaza buffer zones indefinitely, Defense Minister Katz says
item.description: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz says troops will indefinitely remain in the buffer zone they created in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian prime minister meets with Trump on tariffs
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is expected to speak for Italy and the European Union as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Dog reunites with Israeli family after disappearing for 18 months in Gaza
item.description: BINYAMINA, Israel (AP) — Rachel Dancyg never thought she would see her dog again after it disappeared in the Hamas attack that sparked the ongoing war with Israel. Her ex-husband and brother were abducted from Kibbutz Nir Oz in southern Israel and killed.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A Berlin doctor has been charged with the killings of 15 patients under palliative care
item.description: A German doctor has been charged with murdering over 15 people under palliative care; he has also been accused of attempting to cover up the crimes by starting fires in their homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Soros v Trump: Socialists targets conservatives in upcoming European nation's election
item.description: Ahead of elections in May, Albania’s PM Edi Rama has been accused of targeting opposition candidates. One opposition figures says the influence of President Trump, George and Alex Soros will be felt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli airstrike hits hospital entrance in Gaza, killing medic and wounding 9 other people
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit the northern gate of a field hospital in the Gaza Strip on Tuesday, killing a medic and wounding nine other people, a hospital spokesman said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump floats deporting 'homegrown criminals' to El Salvador
item.description: Trump has suggested shipping off U.S. citizens who commit violent crimes to places like El Salvador's notorious mega-prison CECOT.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas says it's sending a delegation to Qatar to continue Gaza ceasefire talks
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hamas official said Monday that the Palestinian militant group is sending a delegation to the Gulf state of Qatar to continue indirect ceasefire talks with Israel over the war in Gaza, as the territory’s Health Ministry said that 38 people were confirmed dead over
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Russia claims its deadly attack on Ukraine's Sumy targeted military forces as condemnation grows
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Russia on Monday claimed its deadly missile attack on Ukraine's Sumy that killed and wounded scores including children had targeted a gathering of Ukrainian troops, while European leaders condemned the attack as a war crime.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WGN 9
